data.description: OctahedronHero.tsx (900 LOC) and three.js currently load eagerly on the home page, inflating first-paint cost. Goal: dynamic-import three + the OctahedronHero scene so the hero ships a lightweight placeholder first and hydrates the WebGL after critical content paints. Mobile-first — phone bandwidth is the constraint that matters.\n\nWhy: home page is the entry point; first-paint speed is a major UX lever especially on cellular.
data.title: Lazy-load WebGL octahedron hero to shrink initial bundle

data.body: User-provided screenshot (3G throttle, iPhone viewport) shows DOMContentLoaded 13.3s, Load 1.2 min. Worst offenders: skyline4.png (1.4 MB, ~60s), three-vendor (233 kB, 36s), 8 eagerly-loaded face JPEGs (~1 MB). Also: hero-poster.jpg returns 200/text-html (does not exist), favicon.ico returns 200/text-html (confirms FRAC-180). Full diagnostic in notes/task_01KTQCBDCQ4RYRSWVAFKQZYWX7.md — scope likely needs to split into 4 tasks.

All acceptance criteria met; no rework needed. Verified independently from impl worktree.

VERIFICATION:
- pnpm typecheck: clean
- pnpm build: clean (2779 modules, 3.54s)
- dist/index.html modulepreload entries: only react-vendor + vite-preload-helper (FCS and three-vendor absent — confirmed via grep)
- dist/_headers: only 2 script preloads for react-vendor + vite-preload-helper (no FCS, no three-vendor, no hero-poster — confirmed via grep)
- pnpm test: 141 passed / 7 failed — the 7 failures are IDENTICAL to master HEAD (buttons.test.tsx x2, footer.test.tsx x2, navigation.test.tsx x1, neighborhood.test.tsx x2), zero new failures introduced

CODE QUALITY:
- vite.config.ts comment block is explicit and load-bearing — explicitly tells future agents not to re-enable preload unconditionally, references HeroPlaceholder.tsx as the visual gap-filler. Excellent breadcrumb.
- HeroPlaceholder.tsx sizing (width: min(90vmin, 550px); aspectRatio: 3/4) matches FractalCityScene canvas hit-target on line 88 EXACTLY — no layout shift when WebGL hydrates. aria-hidden, pointer-events-none, 0.4 opacity sketch feel — all correct per plan.
- heroNavNodes.ts extraction is clean; OctahedronHero.tsx re-exports OUTER_NAV_NODES so any external importer keeps working. Only internal callsite (Hero.tsx) updated to the three-free path.
- Inter weight 600 retention (follow-up commit 5d54127) is a sound call: grep confirms font-semibold is used across 11 shadcn UI primitives (alert-dialog, card, sheet, drawer, dialog, badge, menubar, toast, dropdown-menu, select, context-menu) — all with default sans (Inter). Dropping 600 would have caused faux-bold rendering on every modal/menu in the app. Caught and fixed pre-review.
- Fraunces 300..500 narrowing: verified zero font-bold/semibold/extrabold/black callsites paired with font-serif anywhere in src/. Safe.

PRD ALIGNMENT: Mobile-first goals (FRAC-22.md L172) directly served — dropping ~900KB three-vendor + ~150KB unused font weights from mobile critical path is a textbook mobile-first perf win.

MINOR NOTES (no action):
- heroNavNodes.ts duplicates the housePalette helper (4 lines) to avoid pulling three into the entry chunk. Acceptable; documented in the file.
- The on-3G DCL improvement is not directly verified in CI (would need real network throttle); manual verification deferred to deploy preview per plan.
